repository_ssim: St. Catherine's Monastery of the Sinai
full_text_tesim: Each side of the bifolio preserves 22–23 lines of text, with 7 mm between and arranged in a single column
Prickings are still visible in the outer margin of f. 80, their shape elongated rather than round, and likely made with a penknife. The ruled lines correspond to Sautel-Leroy type 24D1
The upper margin measures 35 mm, the outer 45 mm, and the lower 35 mm (f. 85v). The original manuscript measured approximately 190x130 mm

Μinuscule "rectangulaire aplati ou écrasé" | Text written in an upright rectangular calligraphic minuscule with thick and pronounced strokes hanging from the ruled line. A notable peculiarity is the letter ζ "a proboscide", in the form of a three inclined to the left. This mode of writing is reminiscent of Salentine manuscripts written in "style rectangulaire aplati ou écrasé"
